title: Does structured data on thin location pages help or hurt crawl efficiency?
summary: Working on a local service business site with lots of pages and county location pages. Most of these pages are relatively thin service description, area-specific content, NAP and FAQ schema. Each page has LocalBusiness and FAQ structured data implemented via RankMath. The question I keep coming back to: does adding structured data to thin pages actually help Googlebot prioritize crawling them, or does it add render weight that slows crawl efficiency on pages that are already borderline in terms of content depth? /u/Flat-Ad-1089
